description: Landing page that greets visitors with the Laravel title and conditionally shows navigation links based on authentication status. It displays the app’s locale in the HTML tag, checks if login/register routes exist, and then renders “Home” for authenticated users or “Login” and “Register” for guests. Users can click these links to navigate to the home dashboard, the login form, or the registration form.

description: Inner chat view that displays a scrollable conversation between the logged‑in user and a selected recipient. It shows a back button with the recipient’s name, then iterates over **$chatDetails**, rendering each **$message** (text, sender name, timestamp) left‑aligned for incoming messages and right‑aligned for the current user (**$senderId**). User initials act as avatars, and timestamps are formatted via Carbon. The user can read the full chat history and return to the previous screen via the back link.

description: Sidebar chat user list view that displays a searchable roster of friends. It includes a back button, a text input with search/clear icons, and a scrollable list where each user is rendered with a colored initial badge, tooltip, and data attributes (id, status, username). The view iterates over the **$users** collection, showing each user’s name and ID. Users can filter friends via the search box and click a user entry to open a private chat.

description: Dashboard view confirming login status and showing a user‑specific image and application list. Displays a success alert from `session('status')`, the text “You are logged in!”, and the uploaded `Landing.png` image. Includes a “rotate” button that triggers a JavaScript `rotateFoo()` function for the image. Below, a table (`#userApplicationsTable`) is prepared to list dynamic rows of ID, USER_NAME, APPLICATION_ID, SENT_ON, and STATUS for the logged‑in user.

description: Inventory overview table showing kit stock per scheme. The view renders a striped, hoverable table with columns for SR, Scheme, Batch, Total Indented, Received, Damaged, Destroyed, Missing, Utilized, Available Sales, Available Branch, and Total Available. Data is populated from **$delightKitInventoryDetails**, iterating each scheme code and its inventory metrics. Each numeric cell is a clickable `<a>` (class `kitDetails`) carrying the scheme ID and a `status` attribute, enabling users to open detailed status dialogs or modals for that scheme.

description: Branch inventory view that lets users select a branch and view its stock details. The page loads a custom stylesheet, then presents a dropdown (`<select>`) populated with `$branches` (branch IDs) using Laravel’s Form helper. When a branch is chosen, JavaScript (not shown) fills the `#inventoryDetails` container with the selected branch’s inventory data. Users can filter inventory by branch via the dropdown.

description: Kit Count Approval dashboard that lists kit‑count records in a searchable table (`#kitCountApprovalTable`). Users can filter the list by Sol ID, Delight Scheme Code, DR status and a sent‑date range using the text input, two dropdowns and a date picker. A role‑specific button (shown only for role 16) triggers actions such as “Approve” or “Export”. The view dynamically populates the scheme and status options (`$delightSchemeCodes`, `$drStatusList`) and displays the filtered kit data for approval or further processing.

description: Kit Details History page that lists past kit records in a full‑width table (`#kitDetailsHistoryTables`). Users can filter the list by selecting a Delight Scheme Code, entering Branch ID, Kit No., or Customer ID, and then view the matching rows. An “Export Excel” icon lets users download the filtered results. The view pulls dynamic data for the scheme dropdown (`$delightSchemeCodes`) and pre‑fills the Branch ID (`$branchId`). Primary actions: apply filters, browse the history table, and export the displayed data as an Excel file.

content: ID SOL ID Schema Code Kit Number Customer Id Account Number
description: Kit dispatch interface that lets users filter and send kits for a specific delivery receipt. The view shows a hidden **dr_no** value, three text inputs for **Kit Number**, **Customer ID**, and **Account Number**, and a **Dispatch** button. Below the filters, a responsive table (**#kitDetailsTable**) lists the matching kit details (e.g., ID, kit number, customer info) pulled from the `$dr_no` and related query results. Users can enter filter criteria, click **Dispatch** to process selected kits, and view the resulting kit list in the table.

description: Kit details view that lets users filter and export a list of kits. It displays a toolbar with an “Export Excel” icon, a dropdown populated from **$delightSchemeCodes** (selected **$schemeCodeId**), and text inputs for Branch ID (pre‑filled with **$branchId**), Kit No., and Customer ID. Below the controls a full‑width table (**#kitDetailsTable**) loads the matching kit records. Users can refine the kit list via the filters and download the current results as an Excel file.

Login landing page for the DCB – CUBE – AIS portal. Shows the company logo and a three‑slide carousel on the left side, while the right side (not shown) contains the authentication form. The view pulls the current application version from `config('version.VERSIONS')` for potential display, includes meta tags, favicon, Google font, Bootstrap and custom CSS, and sets a random cookie identifier. Users can view the carousel and proceed to enter credentials to sign in.

User authentication page that presents a branded login screen split into two columns. The left column shows the DCB logo and a Bootstrap carousel of promotional images; the right column (not fully shown) contains the login form with email/username and password fields, CSRF token, validation error messages, and a “Sign In” button plus optional “Forgot Password” link. It dynamically injects the application version name from the `config('version.VERSIONS')` array and a random cookie meta tag. Users can enter credentials and submit to authenticate.

description: A searchable “Seek Approval” page that lets users filter Delight kits before requesting approval. The view loads custom styles, then presents a filter row with dropdowns populated from **$delightSchemeCodes** and **$delightKitStatus**, plus text fields for Kit No., Customer ID, and Account No. Below the filters a full‑width table ( #kitDetailsTable ) displays the matching kit records. Users can select criteria, view the resulting kit list, and initiate an approval action from the table.

Account‑opening “Special Declaration” form view that injects a label helper to render scheme‑specific field titles (entity details, address proof, etc.) and populates dropdowns/toggles for Lead Generated, Distance From Branch, Customer Meeting Location, and Reason for Account Opening using constants and session data. It displays dynamic values such as $accountDetails['scheme_code'] and $entityDetails, allows the user to select or enter the required declaration information, and submits the data for further processing.

description: Dispatch applications view that lists bank dispatch records in a full‑width table (`#dispatchApplicationsTable`). Users can filter the list via a “Customer Name” dropdown (populated from `$customerNames`) and a “Sent Date To” input with a clear‑date icon, then trigger a reload with `getUserApplications`. A “Close Filters” link hides the filter panel. The page hides vertical scrolling and provides a responsive card layout for viewing and managing the filtered dispatch data.

Breadcrumb navigation component for a multi‑step bank onboarding flow. It generates step labels (e.g., “Basic Details”, “OVD Documents”, “CIDD”, “Initial Funding”, “Nomination”, “Customer Declarations”, “Review & Submit”) and conditional click handlers (`redirectUrl`) based on the current `$page`, `$step`, `$is_review`, `$formId`, and `$accountType`. The view displays each step as a clickable link when it is not the active step, allowing users to jump back to previously completed sections.

description: A read‑only OVD submission section that renders a styled table of personal details for each applicant (primary, HUF manager or subsequent HUF members). It conditionally titles the block (“PERSONAL DETAILS: KARTA/MANAGER”, “PRIMARY APPLICANT”, etc.) and lists fields such as the applicant’s name (and, when not HUF, the short name on card). Dynamic variables like $customer_type, $is_huf_display, $i and $huf_display populate headings and row labels. Users can only scroll through the information; no input or actions are provided in this view.

description: Delight Kit details card that lets users view and edit the “Kit Sequence Number” (populated from **$delightKits** with the current **$kit_number** selected) and related fields such as Customer ID. The view renders a Bootstrap card with a subtitle, a Form‑select dropdown, and hidden save icons that appear on change. Users can change the kit number or other fields, then click the floppy‑icon to update the corresponding **account_details** record.

Laravel Blade view for adding or editing a nominee applicant. It renders a form populated with existing nominee data (e.g., $nominee_name, $relatinship_applicant, $nominee_dob, address fields, guardian details, and optional witness declaration). The form includes input fields, dropdowns for relationship and location, date pickers, and submit/cancel buttons. Users can review, modify, or create nominee information, which is then saved to the application record.

description: Initial Funding selection view that lets users choose how the account will be funded. It displays a subtitle “Initial Funding” and three radio options – Cheque, NEFT/RTGS, and a conditional third method (shown only for ETB customers, non‑minor, account type 3). The selected option is pre‑checked from $initial_funding_type, may be disabled via $disabled, and in review mode the option images are blurred. Users can set or change the funding type before proceeding.

Laravel Blade view for adding OVD (One‑View‑Document) applicant details. It renders a form that captures personal information (names, gender, religion, marital status), identity proof selection (Aadhaar, Passport, Voter ID, Driving Licence) with associated numbers and expiry dates, permanent and current address proofs, and signature fields. Dynamic variables such as $customerOvdDetails, $account_id, and $is_review control pre‑filled values, read‑only states, and visibility of buttons. Users can upload/enter documents, edit address data, and submit or save the applicant’s OVD record.

description: Declaration form for account setup, presenting a series of declaration check‑boxes and file‑upload fields (minor, vernacular, name mismatch, PEP, treasury, annexure, third‑party, cash, zero‑balance, other proofs, delight‑kit photograph, acknowledgement receipt). It pre‑populates values from `$userDetails` (e.g., `gpa_required`, `two_way_sweep`, `account_number`, `customer_id`) and respects session state (read‑only/disabled flags). Users can select required declarations, attach supporting documents, and submit the form to save or update their account declarations.

description: Risk classification entry view for an account holder, displaying a pre‑filled form that lets users review and edit the client’s risk details. The page pulls dynamic values from $userDetails (e.g., annual_turnover, source_of_funds, expected_transactions, inward_outward, approximate_value, source_others_comments) and account‑specific data such as AccountIds and accountCountries. Key elements include input fields/select boxes for turnover, fund sources (with “Other” comments), transaction expectations, direction (inward/outward), and approximate value, plus hidden controls for display toggling. Users can modify these fields and submit the form to save or update the risk classification.

Mask/Unmask toggle widget for form fields. The view injects print‑specific CSS that hides the mask‑unmask container and any elements with the `.maskingfield` class when printing, while revealing `.unmaskingfield` elements and removing image blur. In the UI it renders a right‑aligned button group containing a hidden “Mask” button and a visible “Unmask” button (`#maskfields`, `#unmaskfields`). Users click “Unmask” to reveal previously masked input values (or “Mask” when it becomes visible) before submitting or printing the page.

description: Add‑Nominee Details view for a term‑deposit account. Displays a breadcrumb navigation (page 5) and renders one or two nominee entry forms depending on `$accountType` (single for most accounts, double for type 4). Each form includes fields for nominee name, address, relationship, DOB, age, guardian info, and passbook name, pre‑filled from `$nomineeDetails` when available. In review mode (`Session::get('is_review')`), inputs become read‑only and the submit button is disabled. Users can enter or edit nominee information and submit the form to save or update the nominee records.

The view presents a heading with the current AOF number and a hidden “copy to clipboard” icon, an input field for entering an AOF number, a dynamically‑populated dropdown of customer names (using the **$customerNames** collection), and a “Track” button to submit the query. Upon submission, matching AOF details are displayed in a table below.

description: Add OVD Documents view for capturing and reviewing required customer documentation. Displays a dynamic form populated with session‑derived data (customer type, role, number of account holders, scheme code, existing $customerOvdDetails, $accountDetails, $AccountIds). Includes fields for proof‑of‑address, passport/driving licence issue/expiry dates, signature type, and account‑holder selection. Users can upload files, edit dates, and submit or save the document set; the form adapts to ETB or non‑ETB flows and respects read‑only or disabled states.

Scheme declaration view that shows a specific declaration block (e.g., third‑party approval, acknowledgement receipt) with a conditional image‑proof preview. It renders the uploaded proof image (or hides the section when none exists), applies a blur overlay in review mode, and toggles visibility based on redo status, HUF account type, and declaration ID. Users can view the current proof, upload/replace the image, and, when reviewing, see a blurred version to confirm without editing.

Timeline view that lists each workflow activity for a record, showing the activity name, date, time, elapsed activity time and the user who created it. It iterates over **$trackingDetails**, formats timestamps with Carbon, computes the duration between consecutive entries and compares it to the stored **process_tat** to flag overruns with a warning icon. The UI consists of a header row and a vertical timeline of items; users can only scroll/read the generated activity log.

description: Risk classification “Amount” widget displaying the reviewed tenure amount with status icons and an inline “Edit” link. Shows `$reviewDetails['tenure_amount']` when set (with a red “×” icon) or a green check when absent, and toggles an editable text field (`AddFinancialinfoField`) bound to the `risk_classification_details` table. Users can view the current amount, trigger inline editing via the “Edit” link, and submit a new amount directly in the input box.

Add Account Applicant form view that captures personal and account details for a new customer. Displays input fields such as PAN number, date of birth, marital and residential status, account type, mobile, email, and scheme code, with dynamic values (e.g., $pf_type, $customer_account_type). The form adapts to “review” mode (read‑only, disabled fields) and role‑based restrictions (ETB, HUF), and includes hidden controls for attachment handling and ETB search visibility. Users can enter or edit applicant data and submit it for account creation.

User Authentication
description: Submission view that renders the bank information form and, when the session indicates a review state, disables inputs and shows a “User Authentication” modal. The modal pre‑fills the logged‑in username (read‑only) and requires the user’s password before allowing the submission to proceed. It includes a hidden loader element, custom disabled‑toggle styling, and a close button. Users can review/edit bank details (if not disabled) and must re‑enter their password to confirm the submission.

Toggle selector for contact method in a call‑center declaration form. The view renders a hidden field containing the dynamic **$formId**, a label “EMAIL”, a styled checkbox switch (id togBtn) with on/off spans, and a “PHONE” label. Users can flip the switch to choose between email and phone as the preferred communication channel before submitting the surrounding form. This component visually indicates the current selection and passes the chosen option via the checkbox state.

Submission form view that presents a detailed CIF declaration summary and account‑specific fields before final submission. It loads the company logo and any attached images as inline base64 data, then displays dynamic values such as meeting date, generated lead, branch distance, customer location, account‑opening reason, and a scheme‑dependent “special case” field (e.g., Label Code, Elite Account Number, HRMS Number). Users can review these populated details and confirm/submit the form via the provided action button.

description: Customer Enquiry modal that presents a scrollable grid of Delight account details. When invoked, it renders a Bootstrap modal titled “Customer Enquiry View” containing a table with columns such as Sr., Account No, Type, Active, Clear Balance, PAN Card, etc., populated from the `$gridDataDetails` (or `$details`) collection. If no records exist a “No records found” message appears. Users can only view the data and close the modal via the X button; no editing or submission actions are provided.

Risk classification entry form for a new applicant, showing pre‑filled values from $customerOvdDetails, $riskDetails, $checkEtbNtb and $userDetails. The view renders a series of input controls (dropdowns, text fields, radio buttons) for basis of categorisation, customer type, country, citizenship, education, income, net‑worth, occupation, PEP status, comments, US‑person flag, TIN and risk rating, plus a submit/save button. Users can review, edit, or add classification data and submit it for processing.

Entity office‑address KYC form for corporate accounts. Shows pre‑filled fields (entity name, address lines, landmark, country/state/city, pincode, mobile, email) and dynamic labels retrieved via `labelCode` based on the scheme code. Includes a proof‑of‑address dropdown, card‑number input, and front/back image upload controls populated from `$entityDetails`. Users can edit/complete the address information, attach required documents, and click “Next”/submit to save the entity’s office address data.

description: Aadhaar masking dashboard view that presents the selected document’s metadata (CRF number, client name, document type) and prepares the associated image for client‑side processing. Hidden inputs store context values (side, multiside, doc_id, amenddoc_id) and the image URL with a flag indicating its presence. The page’s primary purpose is to load these details so JavaScript can display the Aadhaar image and enable masking actions, without direct form controls in this snippet.

Laravel Blade view that lists each proof document for a case, showing the evidence title, a “‑” toggle button, and a preview of the attached file. If the file is an image it’s displayed blurred (unblurred via the toggle); if it’s a PDF a PDF icon with a download/open link is shown. The view also conditionally renders a QC‑review switch (visible to roles 19‑22) based on existing comments and the case status, allowing the user to mark the document as reviewed.

L3 Updates card that lists five numbered NOTE fields. For each note it loads any existing comment from $amendL3Images, shows the comment in a textarea and, if a comment exists or the user’s role isn’t 22, renders the textarea as read‑only/disabled and hides the edit controls. When editable (role 22), users can type or modify the note text; the view also formats and displays the comment’s creation date. This component lets authorized users view or update up to five L3 update notes.

The view does not display data tables, only these dynamic buttons, allowing the user to trigger the corresponding status change (clear, review, hold, reject) for the current item.

description: Right‑hand details panel that iterates through **$getCrfDetails** and renders each field as a labeled row when its `display` flag isn’t “none”. It shows the field’s display name, current value (or old value for OVD sections), and conditionally applies CSS classes based on `dataLableshow`. The view also incorporates amendment validation (`$amendValidation`) and QC review data (`$qcReviewDetails`) to enable users to review, edit, or flag individual customer data items directly within the panel.

Amendment L1 review edit view that renders a dynamic list of editable fields defined by **$amendL1Rules**. Each rule generates an inline input (text, date, number or pincode) styled with the “saveField” class and an adjacent pencil‑icon (**.editComment**) for toggling edit mode. JavaScript parses the rules into **_L1_editFields**, builds the appropriate HTML template, and injects validation attributes. Users can modify field values directly, see validation cues, and submit changes without leaving the page.

Role 19 users (except when the CRF status is 35) see an editable form (included from amendreviewedit). description: Modal view presenting two confirmation dialogs—**Hold** and **Reject**—that ask the user to confirm placing a form on hold or rejecting it. Each dialog shows a message with a dynamic title span, a required comment textarea (`#hold_comment` / `#reject_comment`), a red “Confirm” button (`#hold` / `#reject`) and a blue “Cancel” button. A third large modal (`#upload_note`) is defined for uploading notes, containing a close button and an extra‑large dialog container. Users can add comments and confirm or cancel the hold/reject action.

Users can type an AOF number into the top‑right input to filter the rows and click the “Export Excel” icon to download the currently displayed data as an .xlsx file. description: A view for importing archival records via Excel. It presents a file‑input labeled “Import Excel” with a **Submit** button that triggers parsing of the uploaded sheet. After submission a hidden table (initially `display‑none`) is revealed, showing the parsed columns – BOX BARCODE, FILE BARCODE, AOF NUMBER, CUSTOMER NAME, CUSTOMER ID, ACCOUNT OPENING DATE, ACCOUNT ID, etc. The user can then **Confirm** to save the data or **Cancel** to abort. The interface therefore lets users upload an Excel file, preview its contents in a dynamic table, and either confirm or discard the import.

description: A searchable table view that lists all CRFs for amendment tracking. It displays each record’s ID, creation date, CRF number (as a clickable link), customer name, and status (converted from config values). The CRF number link triggers the JavaScript function trackingcrf() to open detailed tracking information. Users can scan the list and click any CRF number to view its amendment history or status details.

Amend image‑upload widget that presents a labeled evidence field ( {{ $value['evidence'] }} ) with a mandatory “*” indicator ( {{ $mandatorySign }} ). If an existing file is stored (checked via $getEvidenceData and $value['storageExist'] ) it shows a preview thumbnail or PDF link using the generated URL; otherwise it marks the image as missing. The user can select a new image/PDF via the file input tied to amend_card‑{{ $key }}, replace the current file, and the component updates the hidden storage path for later submission.

description: Amend Customer Account view that presents a step‑by‑step review screen for updating a client’s details. It includes the breadcrumb navigation (`amendbreadcrum`), a header row showing dynamic fields such as **Customer ID** (`$cust_acctNo`), **Account Number** (`$acctNo`, shown/hidden via `$displayAcc`), and **Customer Name**. The page is styled as a card within a centered container, allowing the user to verify the displayed data before proceeding to edit or submit the amended account information.

Amend‑item view that renders a series of grouped checklists. For each $getValue[$j] group it displays the type3 title, then iterates through its items, outputting a checkbox with the item’s description. Checkboxes are dynamically pre‑checked or disabled based on the item’s active, selected flags and on conditions such as $voltMatch, $getEycData, $getVkycStatus and current customer details. Users can select (where not disabled) the applicable items to amend.

Mask/Unmask toggle UI for form fields. The view injects print‑specific CSS that hides the mask‑unmask button container and any elements with the `.maskingfield` class when printing, while revealing `.unmaskingfield` elements and removing image blur. On screen it renders a right‑aligned button group containing a hidden “Mask” button and a visible “Unmask” button (`#maskfields`, `#unmaskfields`). Users can click “Unmask” to reveal previously masked data (e.g., personal identifiers) and later re‑mask it with the hidden button, enabling privacy‑aware data entry and printable output.

Amendment workflow breadcrumb bar that visualises the four‑step process (Customer Selection → Amendment → Documents Upload → Review & Submit). It reads the `$breadcrumshow`, `$page` and `$step` variables to set `$stepdisabled`, then outputs a colored step box (`blu‑clr`, `gry‑clr`, or step‑X) for each stage. Completed steps are clickable, invoking `redirectUrl()` to jump back; the current step is highlighted, future steps are greyed out and non‑clickable. Users can see their progress and navigate back to any enabled prior step.

Additional KYC details form for editing or adding identity proof information. Shows a dropdown populated from **$idProofList** (selected value from **$additionalData->proofIdData->proof_id**), text input pre‑filled with **$id_number**, and hidden date fields for issue and expiry dates (filled from **$id_issuedateProofId** and **$id_expiryDate**). Users can select an ID proof type, enter the corresponding number, and (via JavaScript) reveal and edit issue/expiry dates before submitting the parent form.

Amendment request form view that presents a user’s CRF number and request date, displays the company logo, and conditionally shows an approval link based on cached URL data. The page includes masked input fields (e.g., email, mobile, Aadhaar, PAN) populated from `$getAmendData`, inline images of uploaded documents via `displayInlineImage()`, and a submit button. Users can review pre‑filled personal details, edit allowed fields, upload supporting images, and submit the amendment request.

Amendment tracking timeline view that lists each activity (status) with its date, time, activity‑time and creator. It iterates over **$getAmendTrackingDetails**, computes the interval between consecutive records, compares the minutes‑difference to the record’s **tat** value, and shows a warning badge (yellow) when the duration exceeds the TAT or a success badge (green) otherwise. The UI is read‑only; users can only scroll through the timeline to review the amendment history and performance metrics.

Dynamic collections $groups and $zonelists populate the selects, and the table shows the filtered records. Users can select a group/zone, view the corresponding items, and toggle each item’s active status directly from the list.

description: Customer onboarding review panel that presents each account holder’s basic details in a tabbed interface. The view generates a navigation list (`<ul class="nav-tabs">`) with dynamic tab titles—“Primary Account Holder” or “Karta/Manager” for the first holder and “Applicant X” or “HUF” for subsequent ones—based on the account’s constitution. Selecting a tab reveals a card containing the holder’s data (e.g., `$customerOvdDetails`, `$accountDetails['account_type']`, `$accountDetails['flow_tag_1']`). Users can switch tabs to inspect each holder’s information; no editing or submission actions are provided in this snippet.

Account details status view that shows the required account numbers (Savings, Current, TD) based on the selected $accountDetails['account_type'] and scheme. It displays a dynamic label (e.g., “Savings & Current”), icons indicating completion, and “Done” or “Create” buttons for each missing entry. Data such as $currentdetails['entity_account_no'], $tdApiShow, and $checktdexist['td_entry_done'] are rendered. Users can click the “Create” button (with the formId) to generate a missing account number, otherwise the view only reports completed items.

It displays the “SIGNATURE UPDATE” label, shows the current status using `$accountDetails['signature_flag']` (a check icon for “Done” or a cross for “Not done”), and, when the flag is not “Y”, presents a read‑only text input preset to “Y” and a comment textarea for the admin to record a reason. description: Delight Details view presenting a read‑only summary of a specific kit. It displays the kit sequence number (`$delightKit->kit_number`), customer ID (`$delightKit->customer_id`), and additional kit/customer fields (truncated in the snippet). The UI consists of a styled card with labeled data rows; no input fields or actions are provided in this section, so the user’s only interaction is reviewing the displayed information.

L3 Updates note panel that lists five numbered “NOTE” fields in a single row. Each note shows any saved `$level3notesimages[$i‑1]->dyna_text` and its creation timestamp (Carbon‑formatted), rendered in a read‑only styled textarea for non‑editable roles (role ≠ 8). Editable users can type into the textarea and click the adjacent “Submit” button, which calls `submitL3Update('note', i)`. The view dynamically toggles read‑only mode, note content, and button visibility based on `$readOnlyView` and existing note data.

Account‑creation step in the account‑details timeline that shows each applicant’s customer‑ID status and lets the user generate missing IDs. It renders a timeline badge, a “Create Customer Id” header, a dynamic comment list (✓ or ✗ icons) built from $checkAccountHolder, and a context‑aware button – disabled, “Create” (with $formId and $customerId attributes), or “Done”. Users can click **Create** to submit the associated form and complete the ID creation process.

Users can toggle switches (when visible), view/approve images, and submit or reject the review.

description: NPC review status view that informs the user whether the submitted form is on **Hold** or **Rejected** and shows the associated comment. It conditionally renders a highlighted heading with the `$hold_reject_comment['comments']` text when `$aofStatus` equals **HOLD** or **REJECTED**, after checking the account’s constitution (`$accountDetails['constitution']`). The page also prepares a “verify‑checkbox” section for further interaction (e.g., confirming review). Users can only read the status message and later interact with the verification checkbox area.

Funding‑status timeline entry that shows whether a customer’s funding has cleared. It renders a “Check Funding” label, a dynamic comment (“Cleared” with a check or cross icon based on $fundingStatus['funding_status']), and a context‑sensitive button: a disabled “Create” when $custIdButtonReqd is true, a “Check” button (id check_funding_status, passing $formId) when funding is not cleared, or a non‑clickable “Done” button when cleared. Users can trigger a funding‑status check or view the cleared state.

description: OAO applications dashboard displaying a searchable, paginated table of all OAO records (`#oaoApplicationsTable`). Users can toggle a filter panel to narrow results by AOF number, mobile number, and customer name (populated from `$customerNames`). Users can filter the list, view detailed rows, and download the current dataset as an Excel file.

Account details timeline view that presents each applicant’s dedupe verification status (e.g., “No Match”, “Pending”, “Failed”) with check‑or‑cross icons, and shows a scheme code, dedupe status flag, and “Delight” indicator. It dynamically builds a comment string and renders a context‑sensitive button – “Create” (disabled), “Update”, or a non‑clickable “Done” – based on `$ddQIDButtonReqd` or `$ddStatusButtonReqd`. Users can review the status icons and trigger the displayed button to submit or update the dedupe check.

It dynamically loads labels (e.g., “Entity Details”, “Land Mark”) via a helper, shows the uploaded proof image (or blurred placeholder), displays the entity’s OVD title, and includes a rotate button to toggle image orientation. Users can view the proof, rotate the image, and expand/collapse the accordion sections to inspect additional entity data.

description: Risk details view for CIDD accounts that presents the applicant’s financial risk information (annual turnover, turnover range, etc.) inside a card layout. It conditionally renders editable fields for users with role 5 or 6, using hidden `formId` and dynamic “checked/disabled” states based on `$is_review` and the presence of `$riskDetails`. Users can review or modify the displayed risk data, toggle verification checkboxes, and submit changes via the surrounding form.

Account‑details status widget that informs the user of the current QC/marking state of a specific account. It dynamically builds a status message (`$comment`) showing “Form Marked”, the next role number, or an error, each accompanied by a check or cancel icon. Based on account type, TD API presence, account number and role, it renders a context‑aware action button – a disabled “Mark”, an enabled “Mark” button, or a “Done” button – allowing the user to submit the form for marking or acknowledge completion.

description: Tab‑based review panel for Overdraft (OVD) applicant documents. Dynamically creates a navigation tab for each new‑customer applicant (using $customerOvdDetails and $accountHoldersCount), labeling the first tab “Primary Account Holder” or “Karta/Manager” for HUF accounts and subsequent tabs “Applicant j” (or “HUF”). An additional “CUBE AOF” tab is appended. Selecting a tab loads the corresponding applicant’s OVD proof content (images, blurred previews) for verification, allowing the reviewer to switch between applicants and view/upload required documents.

description: Dashboard view presenting summary statistics for a user’s financial accounts. It displays a hidden “nonedisplay” section that, when revealed, shows responsive cards (e.g., Saving Accounts) with an icon, label, and dynamic count pulled from $accountsCount['savings'] (and similar variables). The page also applies custom table styling for later data tables. Users can glance at their account totals and, by interacting with the cards (e.g., clicking), navigate to detailed account pages or reports.

description: Customer onboarding view that presents each account holder’s details in a tabbed interface. The navigation tabs are generated from **$accountHoldersCount**, labeling the first tab “Primary Account Holder” and subsequent tabs “Applicant X”. Selecting a tab loads a card showing dynamic data such as **$accountDetails**, **$customerOvdDetails**, and the entity type (Individual/Proprietorship). Users can switch between holders to review their onboarding information; the view also conditionally displays a subtitle based on the account type.

Account‑holder activity timeline view that lists each applicant’s query status. The header rows display **Activity**, **Comments**, **Date**, **Time**, **Created By**, and an **Action** column. For every `$checkAccountHolder` entry the view renders a comment icon (check or cross) based on whether `query_id` exists, along with the applicant number. The right‑most column shows either a “Generate” button (enabled/disabled via `$ddQIDButtonReqd` and `formId`) or a “Done” button, letting the user trigger query generation or acknowledge completion.

Timeline view for updating a customer’s account privileges. Shows a header row with columns — Activity, Status, Update, Comments and Action — and then renders a list of privilege‑change entries for the selected account (using `$customerDetails['id']`, `$customerDetails['query_id']`, `$customerDetails['dedupe_status']`, `$application_status`). Users can review each change, see its current status, add comments, and trigger actions (e.g., approve, reject, edit) via buttons in the Action column.

NPC Declaration review panel that lists each required declaration document for an applicant and lets a reviewer mark it as received. The view renders the declaration title, a “YES/NO” indicator, an optional blurred image preview of an uploaded attachment, and a checkbox (pre‑checked/disabled in read‑only review mode). It uses variables such as $npcdeclarations['declaration'], $blade_applicant, $qcReviewDetails, and $reviewDetails to display dynamic text, attachment paths, and review comments, while allowing the user to toggle the checkbox to confirm receipt.

description: DSA Update view that lists each account field in a row‑based table, showing the current **FIELD**, **VALUE**, **VALUE TO UPDATE**, and a **COMMENT** column, plus an **ACTION** column with buttons (e.g., Save, Reject). The view receives `$accountDetails` and `$form_id` from the controller, renders a hidden form identifier, and includes a copy‑to‑clipboard icon for the AOF number. Users can edit the “value to update” and comment, then trigger the corresponding action for each field.

View that reports the creation status of a Term Deposit (TD) or Savings Funding Transfer (FTR) for a specific account. It dynamically sets a label (“Check TD account Created” or “Savings FTR”), shows a comment with the TD account number, API status, reference number or error icons, and renders context‑aware action buttons – a disabled “Create”, an enabled “Create”, a “Done” badge, or a “Transfer” button – based on the account type and processing flags. Users can initiate a TD creation or fund transfer directly from this panel.

Inline entity‑level edit view that renders a dynamic, inline‑editable form for L1 account details. It pulls field definitions ($l1Rules), applicant count ($accountDetails['no_of_account_holders']), proof‑of‑address data, and reference lists (banks, titles, nominee relations) into JavaScript variables, then generates styled input blocks (date, future‑date, string) with a pencil‑icon “edit” trigger. Users can click the edit icon, modify values directly in the displayed fields, and save changes without leaving the page.

Declarations review card that lists all NPC declaration items (excluding acknowledgement receipt and delight‑kit photograph) by looping through the **$declarations** collection and rendering each via the **npc.npcdeclaration** partial. It also shows an “Any Other Applicable Documents” line, displaying the value of **$reviewDetails['other']** or “NO”. In review mode (**$is_review == 1**) checkboxes are pre‑checked, hidden and disabled; otherwise they remain editable. The view is used for reviewing and optionally confirming declaration data before final submission.

Initial Funding review card that presents a customer’s OVD funding details. It conditionally renders when the funding type is cheque or cash (values 1 or 2) and shows the funding type label, the uploaded cheque image (blurred until signed), and a rotate‑button to adjust image orientation. Dynamic data includes $customerOvd['initial_funding_type'], $customerOvdDetails[0]->cheque_image, and constitution flags for HUF display. Users can view the image, rotate it, and proceed to the next verification step.

It displays a centered table with a dashed border, the bank logo (embedded Base64 image), and placeholder sections for dynamic content (e.g., {{ $title }}, {{ $body }}, {{ $footer }}). description: L3 Report page that lets users filter the report by a start date, clear the date field, and export the displayed results to Excel. The view renders a styled, full‑width table with sticky column headers and word‑wrapped cells, populated from server‑side variables (e.g., $reportData). Key UI elements are the date picker input, a clear‑date icon, and a green “Export Excel” button. Users can adjust the date filter, reset it, and download the current report as an Excel file.

It checks the `$apiqueuedata` collection; if records exist, it shows a centered **EXPORT API‑QUEUE REPORT** button (`#excel‑apireport`). If the collection is empty, it displays a card with the message “No Record Found To Export Data.” Users can either trigger the export or see the no‑data notice.

The interface includes a text‑input with a clear (×) icon for the start date, a sticky‑header styled table placeholder (initially hidden) that will be populated with dynamic report rows, and a prominent **Export Excel** icon/button. Users enter or clear a date, trigger the report load, view the generated table, and can download the displayed data as an Excel file.

The view renders a row of colored summary cards (e.g., “New AOF”, “In‑Discrepancy”, “Approved”, “Rejected”) each with an icon, a dynamic count element (`#new_aof_pending_count`, `#desc_aof_pending_count`, etc.) populated via JavaScript/AJAX. <script>
function rsenc(data, publicKey) {
    var publicKey = document.getElementById('pubki');    
    if(publicKey && publicKey != null){
        publicKey = decPubStr(_encPubStrB)+publicKey.value+decPubStr(_encPubStrE);
        const f_publicKey = KEYUTIL.getKey(publicKey);
        // Encrypt the data using public key
        const encryptedData = KJUR.crypto.Cipher.encrypt(data, f_publicKey);
        const encryptedBase64 = hextob64(encryptedData);
        return encryptedBase64;
    }else{
        alert('Encryption Failed!');
        return false;
    }
}

function decPubStr(_i='') {
  let _d = [];
  for (let i = 0; i < _i.length; i++) {
    const xv = _i[i]; const flpd = parseInt(xv, 16);
    const cc = ~flpd; const _o = String.fromCharCode(cc);
    _d += _o;
  }
  return _d;
}
</script>

metadata.purpose: RSA public-key encryption of PAN and other sensitive fields

description: The `rsenc` function performs RSA public-key encryption of sensitive data (e.g. PAN numbers) on the client side. It reads the RSA public key from the hidden `#pubki` HTML element, reconstructs it by calling `decPubStr()` to decode obfuscated key prefix/suffix strings, then uses the jsrsasign library (`KEYUTIL.getKey`, `KJUR.crypto.Cipher.encrypt`, `hextob64`) to encrypt the input `data` and return it as a Base64 string. If the public key element is missing, it alerts 'Encryption Failed!' and returns false. It is defined as inline JavaScript in blade templates and called before AJAX requests to protect PAN numbers in transit.
